title: malus dasyphylla var. reneta
intro: Malus dasyphylla var. reneta, also known as Reneta crabapple, is a variety of the Malus dasyphylla species, a type of flowering plant in the rose family. The Reneta crabapple has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and aid in weight loss, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the Reneta crabapple can be consumed as a tea, used in tinctures, or made into a juice, although its uses and effectiveness should be discussed with a healthcare professional before use.
